methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Akaer is a growth-stage space and aerospace company that has raised USD 37.7M in government funding. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Standard triangulation methods prove inapplicable here. Peer-median valuation cannot be executed: while 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, the narrow filter for growth-stage companies with comparable valuation tiers yields fewer than 3 datapoints, falling below the minimum threshold for statistical reliability. Public-comp anchoring using the sector P/S median of 53.2x across the 6 public tickers is not feasible due to absence of Akaer's revenue on file—a constraint common to private companies. Attempting sector-wide median multiples would introduce material error given the wide dispersion in Space & Aerospace valuations and business models.

This analysis does not support a quantified valuation range. Meaningful triangulation would require either: disclosure of Akaer's revenue or EBITDA to enable public-comp anchoring; identification of 3+ private peer financings at comparable stage and capital raised; or alternative data (user growth, contract backlog, capex intensity). The government-funding source and growth stage position Akaer within the sector, but insufficient comparable transactions and no revenue disclosure preclude analytical synthesis at this time.
